Sequencing note (resolves the file-existence review findings): `docs/your-first-change.md` does not exist at plan time — it is created by story **fat-leaflike-canary**, which this story `depends_on` (edge recorded in the store). Every edit this story makes to that file executes only after that story lands; in dependency order the target exists.

## What

- **Declarative config path (never a manual push):** add `requireSignedOffBy = true` to the `[receive]` section of `infra/gerrit/project.config` and roll out via the existing `setup-project.sh` flow to `refs/meta/config` (JoeOakhartNava admin identity). Rationale: `setup-project.sh` overwrites `project.config` on every run; a one-off manual push would be silently reverted. **Rollback:** revert the line, re-run `setup-project.sh` — documented in a comment beside the key.
- **Two distinct sign-off rules (resolves the identity-mismatch finding):**
  1. **Agent commits (RebarBotNava / `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` committer):** trailer is exactly `Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart <joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com>` — the responsible human's real name with his registered plus-addressed mailbox (routes to the human; kernel practice accepts plus-addresses; the certifier is the human, satisfying DCO 1.1). Documented in CLAUDE.md's commit guidance verbatim.
  2. **e2e test scripts** (`infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh`, `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh`, `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh` — all push to `refs/for/*` and would be rejected post-flip): each script adds `-s` to its `git commit` invocations so the sign-off is the script's OWN configured test identity (a hardcoded human trailer would mismatch the test committer). No other trailer engineering.
- **Automation inventory (decision rule):** enumerate commit-creating automation with `rg -l "git commit" infra/ scripts/ .github/ Makefile` at execution; the three e2e scripts above are the known set; anything newly found is updated the same way (add `-s`) or ticketed via discovered_from.
- CONTRIBUTING.md "Sign your work (DCO)" section, moby/Docker template (https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md "Sign your work"): link developercertificate.org, show the trailer, `git commit -s`, real-name requirement, push-time rejection note, Envoy-style "fixing DCO" note (amend/rebase re-signing), the shepherded-patch two-trailer rule (contributor's own sign-off required in the patch; shepherd adds theirs on amend — coordinated with the PR-posture story), **and a one-line CLA decision record: "rebar uses the DCO, not a CLA; a CLA would be adopted only if Nava counsel requires rights beyond Apache-2.0 §5 + DCO"** (the durable in-repo record of that decision).
- Cross-link retarget INTO `docs/your-first-change.md` (after fat-leaflike-canary lands — see sequencing note): point the tutorial's interim developercertificate.org link at the new CONTRIBUTING DCO section.
- **Migration order (strict, and provable from git history, not tracker artifacts):** commits land in this order — (1) docs (CONTRIBUTING/CLAUDE.md/tutorial retarget); (2) e2e-script `-s` fixes; (3) the `infra/gerrit/project.config` flag + `setup-project.sh` rollout. Because all three are commits in this repo, the order is verifiable post-hoc: the commit touching `infra/gerrit/project.config` must be a descendant of the commits touching the e2e scripts and docs (`git log --oneline -- <path>` comparison). No window where agent/bot pushes fail.
- **Post-flip failure runbook** (one paragraph in the CONTRIBUTING DCO section): rejected push → `git commit --amend -s --no-edit`, re-push; agent-tooling regression → roll back the flag while fixing.
- Flip GitHub's "require sign-off on web-based commits" on the mirror (one settings toggle; belt-and-braces).
- Peer-norm citation for the human-signs policy: Kubernetes AI-review governance (https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/github-management/ai-code-review-tools.md) — cited as supporting practice, not load-bearing authority (the policy stands on DCO 1.1's own terms).

## Scope

IN: `infra/gerrit/project.config` + setup-project.sh rollout, rollback note, CONTRIBUTING.md DCO section (incl. CLA record + failure runbook), CLAUDE.md agent-trailer guidance, e2e-script `-s` fixes, tutorial cross-link retarget (post-dependency), GitHub web-signoff toggle, live verification.
OUT: CLA machinery; the probot/dco GitHub app (acts on PR checks; PRs here are answered/closed by the lockdown workflow the PR-posture story lands); retrofitting existing history (DCO applies to new commits only — kernel/OpenStack precedent); CLAUDE.local.md (gitignored, not a reviewable artifact).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Config flag present and rolled out — prove: `grep -A5 "\[receive\]" infra/gerrit/project.config | grep -q "requireSignedOffBy = true"`; the rollback note exists — prove: `grep -qi "rollback" infra/gerrit/project.config`.
- [ ] Live verification: an unsigned push to `refs/for/main` is rejected (server message captured in the commit message or a `docs/` note) and the same commit re-pushed after `git commit --amend -s` is accepted.
- [ ] e2e scripts sign their commits — prove, per file: `grep -q "commit .*-s\b\|commit -s" infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh`; same grep for `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh` and `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh` (three separate checks); one e2e suite run passes post-flip.
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md DCO section — prove: `grep -q "developercertificate.org" CONTRIBUTING.md`; `grep -q "commit -s" CONTRIBUTING.md`; `grep -qi "not a CLA" CONTRIBUTING.md` (the CLA decision record); `grep -qi "amend" CONTRIBUTING.md` (fix-up/runbook).
- [ ] CLAUDE.md names the exact agent trailer — prove: `grep -q "Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart <joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com>" CLAUDE.md`.
- [ ] Migration order provable from history — prove: the commit touching `infra/gerrit/project.config` is a descendant of the latest commits touching the three e2e scripts and the CONTRIBUTING DCO section (compare `git log` for the paths).
- [ ] Tutorial retarget done after dependency — prove: `grep -q "Sign your work\|DCO" docs/your-first-change.md` links the CONTRIBUTING section (executes post fat-leaflike-canary).
- [ ] GitHub web-signoff on — prove: `gh api repos/navapbc/rebar --jq .web_commit_signoff_required` = true.
finding: The plan says 'Set `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` in `project.config` on `refs/meta/config`' and calls it an 'admin op — requires the JoeOakhartNava identity', but does NOT explicitly state that `infra/gerrit/project.config` (the repo's declarative IaC file) must be edited and the change pushed via `setup-project.sh`. Every prior Gerrit config change in this codebase follows the declarative pattern: edit `infra/gerrit/project.config`, then run `setup-project.sh` to push `refs/meta/config`. If the implementer interprets 'admin op' as a manual SSH/UI change against the live server (without updating the repo file), the autodeploy's detect-only CONFIG_PATHS treatment will flag drift on the next run but never auto-correct it, and the next `setup-project.sh` invocation will overwrite the live enforcement flag with the un-updated repo file — silently disabling DCO enforcement.
location: What section: 'Set `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` in `project.config` on `refs/meta/config` for the rebar Gerrit project (admin op — requires the JoeOakhartNava identity…)'
suggested_fix: In the What section, replace 'Set `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` in `project.config` on `refs/meta/config` for the rebar Gerrit project (admin op — requires the JoeOakhartNava identity…)' with 'Add `[receive] requireSignedOffBy = true` to `infra/gerrit/project.config` in the repo, then push it to `refs/meta/config` by running `setup-project.sh` (the established declarative IaC path used for all prior Gerrit config changes — see `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh`). Requires admin SSH credentials (JoeOakhartNava identity).'

title: Add a CodeQL workflow on the GitHub mirror (python + javascript)
plan:
## Why
Audit OSS finding #2 (MEDIUM). The GitHub mirror has no CodeQL / SAST workflow. Security scanning today is gitleaks-driven secret detection on the Gerrit review path plus a gating pip-audit — good, but no static code analysis. CodeQL is free for public repos and runs fine on the read-only mirror (push/schedule triggers, writes results to the Security tab, does not touch the tree), adding an independent detection layer.

## What / files
Add `.github/workflows/codeql.yml`:
- `languages` matrix: exactly `python` and `javascript` (the CodeQL `javascript` key already covers TypeScript; do NOT use `javascript-typescript`, which is not the Actions matrix key).
- Triggers: `push` to `main` + a weekly `schedule` cron.
- Least-privilege `permissions`: `security-events: write`, `contents: read`, `actions: read`.
- `concurrency` with `cancel-in-progress: true`.
- Pin actions by MAJOR VERSION TAG to match repo convention (test.yml/gerrit-verify use `actions/checkout@v5`, `actions/setup-python@v6`); use `github/codeql-action/init@v3` / `analyze@v3`. No SHA-pinning (no other workflow here uses it).
- Alerts route to the default Security tab.

## Scope
In: `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` (new file only). Out: changing other workflows' pinning; alert routing beyond the Security tab.

Out of scope as an acceptance gate — first-run triage: CodeQL's first scan only runs on the GitHub mirror AFTER this change lands on main, so first-run results cannot exist while the ticket is worked in the worktree. First-run triage is a tracked post-landing operational follow-up (recorded separately once the mirror run completes); it is deliberately NOT an acceptance criterion of this ticket, because it is not evaluable in-session and cannot distinguish a clean run from an errored one at close time.

## Acceptance Criteria (all in-session verifiable)
- [ ] `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` exists and parses as valid YAML (python -c "import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/codeql.yml'))" exits 0), with a `languages` matrix of exactly `python` and `javascript`
- [ ] `permissions` is least-privilege (exactly security-events: write, contents: read, actions: read); a weekly `schedule:` trigger exists in addition to `push:` on main; `concurrency` sets `cancel-in-progress: true`
- [ ] Every `uses:` in the file is pinned by version tag (`@vN`) — grep-verifiable that no `@main`, `@master`, or 40-hex-SHA pin appears
- [ ] The workflow uses `github/codeql-action/init` + `/analyze` (v3) and sets `category`/language matrix correctly (structural check: the two steps are present under the matrixed job)
finding: The repo commits a built JavaScript bundle under `src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor_assets/dist/` (explicitly re-included via the directory's .gitignore). CodeQL's javascript analyzer will scan this vendored/minified bpmn-js output and likely generate a large volume of noisy or false-positive alerts. The plan does not specify any `paths-ignore` glob or `.codeqlignore` file to exclude the dist/ bundle, which conflicts with the AC requirement that 'the first CodeQL run is green and any true-positive findings are triaged'.
location: What / files section; AC line 4 ('first CodeQL run is green'); src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor_assets/.gitignore
suggested_fix:
In codeql.yml under the `analyze` step (or as a top-level `paths-ignore`), add:
```yaml
  paths-ignore:
    - 'src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor_assets/dist/**'
```
Alternatively, add a `.codeqlignore` file at the repo root with that glob.

title: Replication-health + ruleset-drift alerting for the Gerrit->GitHub mirror
plan:
## What
Continuous alerting for the two post-cutover silent-failure modes of the Gerrit->GitHub mirror:
replication failing (GitHub main stops advancing while Gerrit main moves), and the
gerrit-mirror-lock-main ruleset being mutated/deleted out-of-band (drift to unprotected).

## Design (two independent, secretless watchers)
- A tested Python module (src/rebar/mirror_guard.py) holds the verdict LOGIC + I/O + a CLI, driven
  by a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow (the PRIMARY guardian): native GITHUB_TOKEN authenticates
  the GitHub Rulesets API fetch; the Gerrit main SHA is read ANONYMOUSLY (public REST); no new secret.
- A lightweight bash probe in observability.sh is the SECONDARY, house-style replication watcher:
  it compares the anon Gerrit main SHA vs the anon GitHub main SHA (git ls-remote) and publishes a
  CloudWatch metric (no auth, matching the S5 replication probe) -> a Terraform alarm -> SNS.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/rebar/mirror_guard.py exposes replication_verdict(gerrit_sha, github_sha) returning an in-sync/diverged verdict.
- [ ] src/rebar/mirror_guard.py exposes ruleset_verdict(ruleset) enforcing the full lock contract (active, target=branch, refs/heads/main, rules update+deletion+non_fast_forward, bypass = DeployKey only).
- [ ] src/rebar/mirror_guard.py provides thin I/O fetchers for the Gerrit main SHA (anon), the GitHub main SHA, and the GitHub ruleset (GITHUB_TOKEN from env).
- [ ] python -m rebar.mirror_guard runs both checks, prints a JSON result, exits non-zero when unhealthy.
- [ ] Behavioral tests cover in-sync vs diverged for replication_verdict.
- [ ] Behavioral tests cover locked vs each ruleset drift mutation (disabled, missing rule, extra bypass, wrong ref, deleted).
- [ ] Contractual tests pin the verdict output schema.
- [ ] Contractual tests pin the CLI exit codes.
- [ ] .github/workflows/mirror-guard.yml runs the CLI on a cron schedule plus manual dispatch.
- [ ] The workflow grants the job permissions: administration: read so the Actions GITHUB_TOKEN can read the repo ruleset; if org policy disallows that, a fine-grained PAT (administration:read) stored as an Actions secret is the documented fallback.
- [ ] The workflow fails (producing a notification) when either check is unhealthy.
- [ ] infra/scripts/observability.sh publishes the rebar/host:mirror_out_of_sync metric via aws cloudwatch put-metric-data (dimensionless), comparing the anon Gerrit and GitHub main SHAs in bash.
- [ ] infra/terraform/monitoring_ws7.tf defines a CloudWatch alarm on that metric wired to the rebar-gerrit-alerts SNS topic.
- [ ] The guardian is verified LIVE reporting healthy plus locked against production.
- [ ] A simulated ruleset drift is detected by the guardian, then reverted.

## Files
- src/rebar/mirror_guard.py, tests/unit/test_mirror_guard.py, .github/workflows/mirror-guard.yml, infra/scripts/observability.sh, infra/terraform/monitoring_ws7.tf
finding: AC11 asserts that `infra/scripts/observability.sh` 'publishes the rebar/host:mirror_out_of_sync metric via aws cloudwatch put-metric-data (dimensionless), comparing the anon Gerrit and GitHub main SHAs in bash' — but the actual observability.sh contains NO such logic. The file ends at line 119 with voter_errors; there is no `mirror_out_of_sync` metric, no `git ls-remote` call, and no SHA comparison anywhere in the file.
location: AC11 / infra/scripts/observability.sh

title: Cupid enrichment op: LLM ticket-digest extractor + digest schema
plan:
Cupid enrichment op: LLM ticket-digest extractor + digest schema.

## Why
Semantic overlap detection needs a compact, normalized per-ticket representation that
catches paraphrase ("login broken" vs "users can't authenticate") WITHOUT embeddings,
keeping rebar on its Anthropic-only, no-heavy-ML grain. The Cupid pattern
(arXiv:2308.10022) uses an LLM as a one-time feature extractor. DERISKED: the recall
experiment (see epic experiment comment) showed digest-BM25F retrieves 8/8 heavy
paraphrases at rank 1 (recall@20 = 1.0); the digest is the load-bearing artifact for the
whole feature.

## What
A new LLM operation `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` returning a validated dedup-digest
wrapper, plus its output schema and prompt. It is a **structured single-turn** op:
assemble the ticket's canonical text -> load the prompt ->
`RunRequest(mode="structured", output_schema="ticket_digest", execution_mode="single_turn")`
-> `get_runner(cfg, override=runner).run(req)` (the runner validates the structured
response against the `ticket_digest` schema via `finalize_outcome`/`validate_structured`)
-> the op post-processes the proposition count and returns the wrapper.

Modeled on the structured single-turn pattern in
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py::pass1_chunk` (which builds
`RunRequest(mode="structured", execution_mode="single_turn")`) — **NOT** on
`src/rebar/llm/completion.py` (that is a *gate* op that resolves a read-root and delegates
verdict production to the workflow engine; it contains no `RunRequest`), and **NOT** on
`operations.py::review_ticket` (which uses `mode="findings"`/`execution_mode="agentic"`,
the opposite of what we want). Because `enrich` is single-turn with no file/repo tools it
does **NOT** need `gate_source.resolve_gate_handle` / `gate_read_root` or snapshot
materialization; it builds cfg via `LLMConfig.from_env(repo_root=...)` (or an explicit
`config=`), assembles the ticket text, and runs.

The digest schema (`ticket_digest`):
`{problem_keywords: string[], component_or_area: string, key_entities: string[], propositions: string[]}`;
`propositions` = 2-6 atomic problem/repro statements; logs/stack-traces/code are discarded
by the prompt. The payload is deterministic (no timestamps/uuids) so the stored record is
stable modulo LLM wording.

### Op signature (with a body-injection escape hatch for offline tests)
`enrich(ticket_id=None, *, text=None, repo_root=None, config=None, runner=None)`. Exactly
one of `ticket_id` / `text` must be given (else `ValueError`):
- `ticket_id` given -> the op reads the ticket via the established `rebar.llm` facade
  `from rebar import _reads; state = _reads.show_ticket(ticket_id, repo_root=repo_root)` (the
  same layering-correct pattern used by `completion.py:294`, `operations.py:82`,
  `plan_review/orchestrator.py:168`), then assembles the source text with a TRIVIAL INLINE
  join of `state["title"]` + `state["description"]` + each `state["comments"][*]["body"]`
  ("\n"-joined, skipping empties) directly in `enrich.py`. It does NOT import
  `rebar._engine_support.gates._ticket_text` — no `rebar.llm.*` module imports
  `_engine_support.gates`, and doing so would be the first layering violation in the package;
  the join is a couple of lines and needs no private helper. (S1/`2d0f`, which computes a
  content-hash, owns its OWN local normalizer for hashing; enrich's inline join only feeds the
  LLM prompt and need not be byte-identical to it.)
- `text` given -> the op uses that string directly as the source, bypassing the store read
  entirely. This is the injection seam the offline `test_enrich_quality` and other unit
  tests use to feed a fixture `body` without a live ticket store. (It is a real API on the
  op, not a test-only monkeypatch.)

### System prompt + instructions assembly (query context)
- **system_prompt** is resolved from the packaged prompt FILE `reviewers/ticket_digest.md`
  (file stem `ticket_digest` -> prompt id `ticket-digest`, underscores normalized to dashes
  exactly as `ticket_quality.md` -> `ticket-quality`) via the da27 prompt machinery —
  `prompts.get_prompt("ticket-digest", repo_root=cfg.repo_path)` then
  `prompts.resolve_prompt(prompt, {...}, repo_root=cfg.repo_path)` — mirroring
  `plan_review/passes.py::_resolve_system`. No inline prompt string constants; a project
  `.rebar/prompts/ticket-digest.md` override wins.
- **instructions** are the source text resolved above (from `ticket_id` or the injected
  `text`), rendered as plain text. No graph, no file contents. The PROMPT (not the op)
  instructs the model to discard any logs/stack-traces/code that appear in the body — so log
  exclusion is a prompt-encoded guarantee, verified statically by `test_prompt_excludes_logs`.

## Return shape & processing order (grounded in `findings.finalize_outcome`)
`get_runner(cfg, override=runner).run(req)` returns, for `mode="structured"`, the dict
`{**payload, "runner": <str>, "model": <str|None>, "trace_id": <str|None>}` where `payload`
is the model's structured output already validated against the `ticket_digest` schema
(`findings.finalize_outcome` -> `validate_structured`, `findings.py:321`). So the run result
carries the four digest fields PLUS three provenance keys — it is NOT itself a bare digest.
The op therefore:
1. Selects the digest by field name:
   `digest = {k: run_result[k] for k in ("problem_keywords", "component_or_area", "key_entities", "propositions")}`
   (dropping the `runner`/`model`/`trace_id` provenance keys).
2. Post-processes the `propositions` list ONLY: if `len > overlap_propositions_max`, truncate
   to max; if `len < overlap_propositions_min`, keep them all and set
   `low_proposition_count=True`; it never raises on count.
3. Returns the wrapper
   `{"digest": <ticket_digest dict, exactly the four fields>, "low_proposition_count": bool}`.
   `low_proposition_count` lives on the op's RETURN object only — never a fifth field of the
   persisted `ticket_digest`. The `digest` value validates against `ticket_digest`.

## Error contract (resolves AC "clean error, no partial write")
- A shape-invalid structured payload (valid JSON, wrong shape — e.g. `propositions` missing
  or non-string) raises `FindingsError` from `validate_structured` (`findings.py`) INSIDE
  the runner's `run()`, before the op sees any result; the op never catch-and-partial-writes,
  so no record is persisted. This is the path `test_bad_shape` exercises with a FakeRunner
  seeded with a schema-invalid `structured=` dict.
- An absent structured response at runtime (the live model emits no parseable structured
  object) is raised as `rebar.llm.errors.StructuredOutputError` by `finalize_outcome`
  (`findings.py:305`, `structured is None` branch) — a framework guarantee on the live path
  (FakeRunner cannot reproduce it: `FakeRunner(structured=None)` falls through to the
  findings branch, `runner.py:120-137`), so it is asserted at the `finalize_outcome` unit
  level, not fabricated through FakeRunner.
- Absent LLM (no `[agents]` extra / no API key) raises `LLMUnavailableError`/`LLMConfigError`
  (subclasses of `LLMError`) cleanly via the runner preflight/config path; `test_absent_llm`.
- The op defines NO new exception type — it reuses the framework's (`FindingsError`,
  `StructuredOutputError`, `LLMUnavailableError`).

## Config-bound threading (resolves the zero-arg-builder gap)
The proposition bound is NOT enforced through the `register_contract` schema builder (the
zero-arg `Callable[[], type]` cannot see config). Instead: (a) the prompt instructs the
model to emit MIN..MAX propositions, and (b) the op post-validates the parsed count
(truncate to max if more; keep + flag `low_proposition_count` if fewer; never raise). The
MIN/MAX bounds are LLM-feature tunables on `LLMConfig` (`[tool.rebar.llm]`, env
`REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MIN` / `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MAX`, read in
`LLMConfig.from_env` via the `_llm_int` helper) — `_config_schema.py` reserves the `llm.*`
layer, so these must NOT go on `VerifyConfig`.

## Scope (exact edit targets)
- `src/rebar/llm/config.py`: add module constants `DEFAULT_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MIN = 2` /
  `DEFAULT_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MAX = 6`, dataclass fields
  `LLMConfig.overlap_propositions_min` / `overlap_propositions_max`, and two `_llm_int(...)`
  lines in `from_env` (env `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MIN/MAX`, file keys
  `overlap_propositions_min/max`).
- New op module `src/rebar/llm/enrich.py` defining
  `enrich(ticket_id=None, *, text=None, repo_root=None, config=None, runner=None)` (exactly
  one of `ticket_id`/`text`; `text` bypasses the store read — see Op signature above).
- Export: add `from rebar.llm.enrich import enrich` to `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py` and add
  `"enrich"` to the `# operations` group of its `__all__` (NOT `src/rebar/__init__.py`; the
  core package must not import `rebar.llm`).
- Prompt `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/ticket_digest.md` (stem `ticket_digest` -> id
  `ticket-digest`), front-matter `schema_version: 1`, `execution_mode: single_turn`,
  `outputs: ticket_digest`, `category: enrich` (NOT `review`, so it is not indexed/selected
  as a reviewer — `reviewers/index.json` lists only `category=="review"`), and a body that
  explicitly instructs the model to discard logs/stack-traces/code and emit MIN..MAX atomic
  propositions. The valid `execution_mode` enum is `single_turn|agentic`
  (`prompting/prompts.py:74`); `1-TURN` is invalid. If a new packaged prompt must be
  enumerated in a `_packaged_prompt_files()`/catalog manifest, add `ticket_digest.md` there.
- Output schema `src/rebar/schemas/ticket_digest.schema.json` (all four fields required;
  `problem_keywords`/`key_entities`/`propositions` = arrays of non-empty strings;
  `component_or_area` a non-empty string; `propositions` `minItems: 1`). WIRE IT: in
  `src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py` add `TICKET_DIGEST = "ticket_digest"`, register
  `OUTPUT_SCHEMAS["enrich"] = TICKET_DIGEST`, and add `TICKET_DIGEST` to `__all__` — this
  satisfies `tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_coverage.py::test_every_schema_file_is_wired`
  (every schema file must appear in `OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.values() | exempt`). The synthetic
  `"enrich"` key has no CLI `--output` arm (like `"verify_completion"`), so the CLI-coverage
  invariants do not drive it.
- Contract registration + bootstrap: define the builder `ticket_digest_response_model()`
  **inside `src/rebar/llm/contracts.py`** (co-located with `completion_verdict_response_model`,
  pydantic imported inside the body), and register it at the BOTTOM of `contracts.py` with
  `register_contract("ticket_digest", ticket_digest_response_model)` next to the two built-ins
  (`review_result`, `completion_verdict`). Registration is guaranteed-before-use by
  **co-location**: `response_model_for` is defined in `contracts.py`, so any code path that
  calls `response_model_for("ticket_digest")` has necessarily imported `contracts.py`, which
  executes the module body — including the bottom `register_contract(...)` line — first. (This
  is the correct mechanism; `rebar.llm.__init__` does NOT import `contracts` at module top —
  the runner imports it lazily — so the guarantee is co-location, not startup import.) Defining
  the builder in `contracts.py` (not `enrich.py`) also avoids a `contracts -> enrich` import
  cycle. `enrich.py` merely references the schema by the string name `"ticket_digest"`.

## Dependencies
None (leaf). S1/S2/S7 consume the op + the `ticket_digest` schema as sibling stories.

## Testing
- Unit `tests/unit/test_enrich.py` (all use `FakeRunner(structured=...)` — NO live LLM):
  - `test_schema_valid` — `enrich()` with a FakeRunner seeded with a valid digest returns
    `{digest, low_proposition_count}` and `digest` validates against `ticket_digest`.
  - `test_no_nondeterministic_fields` — the returned `digest` has exactly the four schema
    fields (no timestamps/uuids/provenance keys leaked from the run result).
  - `test_propositions_bounded` — FakeRunner returning >max propositions => truncated to max;
    <min => kept with `low_proposition_count=True`, no raise.
  - `test_bad_shape` — FakeRunner seeded with a schema-invalid `structured=` dict (e.g.
    `propositions` omitted) => `FindingsError` raised inside the run, and nothing is returned/
    written (no partial write).
  - `test_absent_llm` — with the real (pydantic_ai) runner and no `[agents]`/key, preflight/run
    raises `LLMUnavailableError`/`LLMConfigError` cleanly.
  - `test_enrich_exported` — `from rebar.llm import enrich` succeeds AND
    `rebar.llm.contracts.response_model_for("ticket_digest")` returns the registered
    ticket_digest model (not the default findings model) — covers the export + contract-
    registration Scope items.
  - `test_prompt_excludes_logs` — STATIC assertion that the packaged
    `reviewers/ticket_digest.md` prompt text contains the explicit "discard
    logs/stack-traces/code" directive (the honest place the exclusion guarantee lives — a
    FakeRunner cannot prove model behaviour).
  - `test_prompt_frontmatter` — loads the prompt via `prompts.get_prompt("ticket-digest")`
    and asserts `execution_mode == "single_turn"`, `outputs == "ticket_digest"`, and that it
    is NOT a reviewer (`category != "review"`, absent from `reviewers/index.json`) — so the
    enrich prompt is never selected as a plan/ticket reviewer.
  - `test_finalize_absent_structured` — a `finalize_outcome`-level unit asserting that a
    `structured_response=None` outcome raises `StructuredOutputError` (the live-path guarantee
    FakeRunner cannot reproduce).
- Quality fixture test `test_enrich_quality` — **offline + deterministic, exercises the real
  `enrich()` op path** (assembly -> run -> select -> post-process -> wrap). Fixtures live at
  `tests/fixtures/enrich_quality/*.json`, >=6 files COMMITTED as deliverables of this story,
  each shaped
  `{"ticket_id": str, "body": str (one is a ~4000-token body), "digest": {<pre-generated ticket_digest, four fields, captured once from a real enrich run>}, "gold": [<expected entity/keyword strings>]}`.
  The test FIRST asserts cardinality — `assert len(fixtures) >= 6` (so an empty/missing
  fixture dir fails loudly rather than passing vacuously). Then for each fixture it builds a
  `FakeRunner(structured=fixture["digest"])` and calls `enrich(text=fixture["body"], runner=<fake>)`
  (the `text=` injection seam — no store read), and asserts deterministic string-set
  membership on the RESULT: >=1 `gold` string appears (case-insensitive) in
  `result["digest"]["key_entities"]` union `result["digest"]["problem_keywords"]`. This both
  exercises `enrich()` and stays deterministic (the canned digests are real captured model
  outputs; regenerate only when the prompt changes).
- Gated live regenerator `test_enrich_quality_live` — `@pytest.mark.skipif` unless
  `REBAR_RUN_LLM_EVAL=1`; calls the real model over the fixture bodies and re-asserts gold
  membership (the path that regenerates the canned `digest` blocks). NEVER runs in CI.
- Proving command: `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py -q` (self-contained;
  fixtures committed, live test skipped, so it never needs a generation step first).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` returns `{digest, low_proposition_count}`, and the
  `digest` field (exactly the four schema fields) validates against `ticket_digest`;
  `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_schema_valid` and `::test_no_nondeterministic_fields`.
- [ ] `from rebar.llm import enrich` succeeds and `contracts.response_model_for("ticket_digest")`
  returns the registered model (not the default findings model);
  `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_enrich_exported`.
- [ ] The packaged `reviewers/ticket_digest.md` prompt encodes the logs/stack-traces/code exclusion
  directive; `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_prompt_excludes_logs`.
- [ ] The `propositions` count is enforced in the op (>max truncate to max; <min keep + flag,
  no raise); `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_propositions_bounded`.
- [ ] A shape-invalid structured payload raises `FindingsError` with no partial write
  (`::test_bad_shape`); an absent structured response raises `StructuredOutputError`
  (`::test_finalize_absent_structured`); absent-LLM raises `LLMUnavailableError`
  (`::test_absent_llm`).
- [ ] The quality fixture test asserts `len(fixtures) >= 6` (fails loudly if the committed
  fixture dir is empty/missing) and, for each, calls `enrich(text=fixture["body"], runner=<fake>)`
  with a FakeRunner seeded with that fixture's captured digest, asserting >=1 gold
  entity/keyword appears in `key_entities` union `problem_keywords` (deterministic; exercises
  the op path via the `text=` injection seam); `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_enrich_quality`.
- [ ] The packaged prompt loads as `ticket-digest` with `execution_mode: single_turn`,
  `outputs: ticket_digest`, and is NOT a reviewer (`category != review`);
  `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_prompt_frontmatter`.
finding: The `category` field for `enrich.md` is underspecified: the plan says 'pick an existing member of the closed category set' but no existing category fits a feature-extractor prompt — using `category: review` would incorrectly add the prompt to the reviewer catalog (`load_catalog` / `is_reviewer = True`), and the other categories (`code-review-pass`, `plan-review-criterion`, `plan-review-pass`) are equally wrong semantically.
location: Scope section: 'Prompt src/rebar/llm/reviewers/enrich.md, front-matter ... a category value from the prompt library's closed category set (do NOT invent one; pick an existing member)'
suggested_fix: Remove the 'do NOT invent one' constraint and introduce a new category value `enrich` (or `feature-extractor`) to the closed set, adding it to FRONT_MATTER_KEYS documentation. Alternatively, if the prompt should not be registered in the reviewer catalog at all, clarify that `category` may be omitted or set to a sentinel that leaves is_reviewer=False.

title: identity: provider-neutral mapping + Jira adapter (outbound assignee/reporter)
plan:
## Dependencies (STACKED — review with --ref HEAD)
Builds on **ddbe** (identity entity with `mappings: [{provider, external_id}]`),
committed on this branch, not yet on origin/main. Review with `--ref HEAD`.

## Context
Resolve outbound Jira assignee/reporter by the provider's opaque **accountId**,
looked up through the identity entity's `{provider, external_id}` mappings, with
graceful degradation. Mappings key on the opaque id, NEVER email.

## Architectural constraint (drives every placement below)
`acli.py` is the reconciler ENGINE's Jira client: it imports only within the
`rebar_reconciler` package (its own `acli_*`/`adapters`/`adf` submodules) and must NOT
reach up into rebar's CORE library (`rebar._commands.*`) — that layering keeps the
engine separable from rebar core and avoids a circular import (rebar core → engine →
rebar core). (Its docstring's "stdlib only" line is stale re: intra-package imports,
but the core-library boundary holds.) Therefore ALL identity/mapping resolution lives
in the reconciler FLOW layer (`outbound_differ.py`, `dispatch_one.py`, which already
depend on rebar core); `acli.py`/`acli_rest.py` receive only a bool flag + resolved
accountId strings. (Resolution helpers take no `repo_root` at these sites — they
default to rebar core's `config.repo_root()` internally, per ddbe's identity module;
there is NO `repo_root()` in the reconciler's own `config.py`.)

## What
### 1. Provider-neutral seam (`src/rebar/_commands/identity.py`)
- `resolve_mapping(provider, external_id, *, repo_root=None) -> str | None` — id of the
  identity whose `mappings` contains `{"provider":provider,"external_id":external_id}`
  (exact match on the opaque id), else None. (AC1 seam; 2f13 also consumes it.) Never raises.
- `jira_account_id(local_assignee, *, repo_root=None) -> str | None` — resolve a LOCAL
  assignee string to a Jira accountId: an identity whose ticket id == `local_assignee`
  OR whose `email` == it (case-insensitive); return its `{provider:"jira"}`
  `external_id`, else None. Never raises.
- `identity_email(local_assignee, *, repo_root=None) -> str | None` — the matched
  identity's `email` (same id/email matching as `jira_account_id`), so the
  `/user/search` fallback can obtain an email to query. Never raises.

### 2. Assignee accountId fast-path (resolve in the DIFFER, skip flag through to acli)
- `_assignee_resolver` (`outbound_differ.py`, flow layer, may import rebar) tries
  `identity.jira_account_id(assignee)` FIRST. `_assignee_resolver`'s return is
  EXTENDED from `(accountId|None, authoritative)` to a 3-tuple `(accountId|None,
  authoritative, is_account_id: bool)` where `is_account_id` is True ONLY when the
  value came from the identity-mapping fast path (`jira_account_id`). `_diff_fields`
  (the caller) writes the accountId into `changed['assignee']` and sets
  `changed['_assignee_is_account_id'] = is_account_id`. All existing callers of
  `_assignee_resolver` must be updated to unpack the 3-tuple — notably
  `outbound_fields.py` (~line 435, currently `acct, authoritative = assignee_resolver(...)`)
  and any other unpack site — else `ValueError: too many values to unpack`. (`OutboundMutation`
  is a frozen dataclass — the sentinel rides INSIDE the `fields`/`changed` dict, not a
  new dataclass field.)
- `dispatch_one.update_one` POPS `_assignee_is_account_id` off `fields` (a small
  `_update_one_apply_*`-style step) and passes `assignee_is_account_id=<bool>` as a
  kwarg into `client.update_issue`.
- `AcliClient.update_issue` (stdlib-only) gains `assignee_is_account_id: bool = False`;
  it POPS this kwarg before delegating to the module-level `update_issue(**kwargs)` (so
  it is never forwarded to the ACLI subprocess as `--assignee_is_account_id`), and when
  True it submits `assignee` DIRECTLY (skips `validate_assignee_exists`). No rebar
  import enters acli.py — only a bool.
- Degradation UNCHANGED: no mapping → the existing `validate_assignee_exists`
  string-match → `AssigneeNotFoundError` → soft-fail → unassigned/project-default.
  264f never hard-fails on an unresolvable assignee.

### 3. `/user/search` bootstrap (best-effort, TRANSIENT, flow layer)
In `_assignee_resolver`'s fallback (flow layer), when `jira_account_id` misses, the differ obtains the
identity's email via `identity.identity_email(assignee)`
(falling back to `assignee` itself when it already looks like an email); if an email
is available it best-effort calls the client's `/rest/api/3/user/search?query=
<email>` (a helper in `acli_rest.py` that takes an email and returns an accountId or
None). The v3 endpoint returns a JSON LIST of user objects each with `accountId` +
`emailAddress`; extract the `accountId` of the entry whose `emailAddress` matches the
email EXACTLY (case-insensitive). Since Jira substring-matches, ZERO or ≥2 exact
matches → NO result (do not guess). The resolved accountId is used for THIS run only
(NOT written back to `mappings`; persistence out of scope). Any miss/ambiguity/hidden
email degrades to the string-match/unassigned path WITHOUT failing.

### 4. Reporter by accountId — dedicated REST sub-call, resolved in the flow layer
- `dispatch_one.update_one` gains `_update_one_apply_reporter(fields, issue_key,
  client)`, called BEFORE `_update_one_filter_fields` (mirroring
  `_update_one_apply_parent` at line 518→493): it POPS `reporter` off `fields`,
  resolves the local reporter string to an accountId via
  `identity.jira_account_id(reporter)`, and (when
  resolved) calls `client.set_reporter(issue_key, account_id)`. When
  `jira_account_id(reporter)` returns None (the reporter maps to no identity/
  accountId), reporter is SKIPPED (dropped — never sent as a raw string) and a
  soft-fail `alert_store.append(...)` of kind `outbound-reporter-unresolved`
  (`jira_key`, `field="reporter"`, `reason`) is recorded; the sync continues. Because reporter is
  popped BEFORE the allowlist filter, it is NOT added to `_OUTBOUND_BATCH_ALLOWLIST`
  and never reaches the scalar `client.update_issue(**fields)`. `reporter` IS added to
  `outbound_fields.py` so the differ emits it into `changed`.
- `acli_rest.set_reporter(jira_key, account_id)` uses `_direct_rest_put_raw`
  (NOT `_direct_rest_put`, which wraps as `{"value": ...}` for the properties API):
  `_direct_rest_put_raw(f"/rest/api/3/issue/{jira_key}", {"fields": {"reporter":
  {"accountId": account_id}}})`.
- `_update_one_apply_reporter` accesses `alert_store` by LAZY-LOAD (by file path,
  the same pattern `run_differs.py` / `fetcher.py` use) and obtains `repo_root` via
  `rebar.config.repo_root()` (flow layer may import rebar core) — `update_one`'s
  signature is NOT changed. Both soft-fail records below go through this.
- Failure is HTTP-ONLY (no subprocess): catch `urllib.error.HTTPError` (4xx =
  Modify-Reporter not granted) around ONLY the `set_reporter` call, record
  `alert_store.append(...)` kind `outbound-reporter-not-permitted` (fields `jira_key`,
  `field="reporter"`, `reason`), and let `update_one` continue (soft degradation) so
  OTHER fields still apply and the sync does not hard-fail.

## Scope
In: the `resolve_mapping`/`jira_account_id` seam, the assignee accountId fast-path
(differ → sentinel → acli bool), the transient `/user/search` bootstrap, and the
reporter REST sub-call with HTTP-only isolated degradation. Out: inbound ghost minting
(2f13), enforcement (3183), persisting bootstrapped accountIds. Tests use a fixture
identity + a stubbed client (no live Jira).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `resolve_mapping(provider, external_id)` returns the mapped identity id (keyed on
  the opaque id, not email) and None when absent; `jira_account_id` returns the jira
  accountId for a matching local assignee (and None otherwise) and `identity_email`
  returns that identity's email (and None when unmatched) — test all three.
- [ ] With a fixture identity mapping a local assignee to a stubbed accountId, the
  outbound mutation carries that accountId as the assignee and the stubbed client's
  `validate_assignee_exists`/assignable-search is NOT called — asserted by a spy whose
  call-count is 0 (measurable "no Jira search needed").
- [ ] An assignee with no identity mapping and a stubbed assignable-search miss
  degrades to unassigned/project-default WITHOUT raising or failing the sync (test).
- [ ] `/user/search` bootstrap: an identity with an email but no jira mapping resolves
  via a stubbed `/user/search` exact-email match to that accountId (transiently); a
  stubbed miss or ≥2-match degrades without failing (test both).
- [ ] Reporter is applied via `set_reporter` (REST) with the resolved accountId when
  the stub succeeds; when the stubbed `set_reporter` raises an HTTP 4xx, `update_one`
  records an `alert_store` entry of kind `outbound-reporter-not-permitted`, still
  applies other fields, and does not fail the sync (test asserts the alert, other
  fields applied, overall success).

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/_commands/identity.py` (resolve_mapping + jira_account_id seam)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py` (_assignee_resolver: identity fast-path + /user/search bootstrap + _assignee_is_account_id sentinel)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/dispatch_one.py` (pop _assignee_is_account_id → assignee_is_account_id kwarg; _update_one_apply_reporter pop+resolve+set_reporter before the filter)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli.py` (update_issue: assignee_is_account_id bool param, popped before delegation, skips validate_assignee_exists)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_rest.py` (/user/search email→accountId helper + set_reporter via _direct_rest_put_raw)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_fields.py` (emit reporter into changed)
finding: The `/rest/api/3/user/search` endpoint used in Section 3 (bootstrap helper in `acli_rest.py`) is a new endpoint surface never previously used in this codebase, and the plan describes its response as a flat JSON list without acknowledging that Jira Cloud v3 paginates this endpoint. The default page size is typically 50 (controlled by `maxResults`); a query returning more than one page would silently truncate results, potentially causing the '≥2 exact matches → NO result' deduplication rule to apply to a truncated view and falsely conclude 'no exact match' when a second identical entry is on page 2 (e.g., soft-deleted duplicate accounts).
location: Section 3 — `/user/search` bootstrap; `acli_rest.py` new helper
suggested_fix: Add `&maxResults=10` (or similar small cap sufficient to detect ambiguity) to the `/rest/api/3/user/search?query=<email>&maxResults=10` URL in the helper. Since the goal is exactly-1-match detection, fetching more than ~5 results is unnecessary; a small cap also avoids accidentally pulling a large user-list and makes the pagination concern moot for practical email-based queries.

title: Classify LLM failures into the resolution-disposition enum + verbatim diagnostic
plan:
Classify every LLM failure into the closed resolution-disposition enum paired with a sanitized diagnostic (verbatim within a field allowlist), delivered as a PURE, total library function — the vocabulary + mapping the later stories wire in, without changing any exception the runner raises today.

## Why
`src/rebar/llm/runner.py` flattens auth, 429, 529, timeout, and content-filter into one `LLMUnavailableError` — no caller can tell "retry" from "buy quota" from "fix config" from "the agent is looping." Gates, exit codes, messaging, and telemetry need a typed disposition plus the raw truth for troubleshooting. This story delivers that CLASSIFICATION as a side-effect-free function; the per-seam wiring, the exit-code bifurcation, and any NEW raised error types are story authorial-hated-blackbear's (2eca) — it is the named owner of runner.py reconciliation and the only story that changes control flow.

## The multi-seam reality (why this is metadata-only)
The RED baseline (story arawana, committed a63ae39c) empirically pins that failures do NOT all pass through one `except`:
- `UsageLimitExceeded` is intercepted at a DEDICATED prior arm (`runner.py:335`) and re-raised as `LLMRunnerError`, BEFORE the generic `except Exception` (`runner.py:353-365`).
- `content_filter`/`length` `finish_reason` never raise from the model layer — the structured path's `structured.check_stop_reason` raises `UnretryableOutputError` (`structured.py:243`).
- rebar's own typed errors pass through unchanged at `runner.py:351` (`except LLMError: raise`).
- `sizing.is_context_limit_error(exc)` (sizing.py:171) matches on the exception MESSAGE to drive the model-escalation ladder; `pass1_with_ladder` (sizing.py:229) branches on `LLMUnavailableError` vs other.
So this story changes NO raised type anywhere; it adds a classifier + a metadata object. A pure classifier CAN classify a `UsageLimitExceeded` even though the generic seam never receives one — because callers (the RED suite; story drake; blackbear's future wiring) invoke the function DIRECTLY with any signal.

## What — five constructs, all in `src/rebar/llm/failure.py`
- `class ResolutionClass(str, Enum)` — the closed 8-class vocabulary: WAIT_AND_RETRY, RETRY_NOW, INCREASE_PROVIDER_LIMITS, CHANGE_SETTINGS, CHANGE_INPUT, CHANGE_PROVIDER_OR_MODEL, FIX_AGENT_DESIGN, NEEDS_INVESTIGATION.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class LLMOutcome{resolution_class, diagnostic: dict, retryable: bool}` — only `retryable` (True iff class in {WAIT_AND_RETRY, RETRY_NOW}) is behaviour-critical; the class is the messaging/telemetry hint.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class ClassifyContext{model, provider, attempts, trace_id, finish_reason}` — all optional; `classify_llm_failure(exc)` works with none. `finish_reason` lets the structured seam classify a content-filter/truncation signal that raised no exception.
- `def classify_llm_failure(exc, ctx=None) -> LLMOutcome` — the pure, TOTAL classifier (never raises; an unmatched failure or an internal/sanitizer error degrades to NEEDS_INVESTIGATION with a minimal diagnostic).
- `def sanitize_diagnostic(fields) -> dict` — the allowlist + redactor (below).

NO new exception classes are introduced in this story (that is blackbear's, alongside the raise-site + exit-code changes). `errors.py` is NOT edited here. Back-compat is therefore trivial and total: this story raises nothing new, so every existing `except LLMUnavailableError` and every exit-code consumer is unaffected — asserted by a test that classifies each RED signal and confirms `classify_llm_failure` is additive (it returns metadata; it does not raise).

### Signal → resolution_class mapping (the classifier's COMPLETE vocabulary — every row a CONCRETE, matchable artifact)
Callers invoke the function directly with any of these; the RED suite pins each row by direct call, independent of where the classifier is wired.
| Concrete signal (exception type / status+body / finish_reason) | resolution_class | retryable |
|---|---|---|
| `ModelHTTPError` status 429 (body code NOT `insufficient_quota`); 529; 500/502/503/504 | WAIT_AND_RETRY | yes |
| `httpx.ReadTimeout` / other `httpx.TimeoutException` (read/pool/write) | WAIT_AND_RETRY | yes |
| `httpx.ConnectError`; `httpx.ConnectTimeout`; `ModelAPIError` with no `status_code` | RETRY_NOW | yes |
| `ModelHTTPError` 429 body code `insufficient_quota`; status 402 | INCREASE_PROVIDER_LIMITS | no |
| `ModelHTTPError` 401/403; malformed 400 (no context-length hint); `UserError` | CHANGE_SETTINGS | no |
| `ModelHTTPError` 400 with context-length hint (`is_context_limit_error` phrasing); 413; `finish_reason=="content_filter"` (via ctx); `ContentFilterError` | CHANGE_INPUT | no |
| `FallbackExceptionGroup` (provider/model fallback chain exhausted) | CHANGE_PROVIDER_OR_MODEL | no |
| `UsageLimitExceeded` (diagnostic records request- vs tool-call-limit); `IncompleteToolCall` | FIX_AGENT_DESIGN | no |
| anything else | NEEDS_INVESTIGATION | no |

- Matching is by concrete type / `status_code` / body `error.type|code` / `finish_reason` ONLY — no vague "blip"/"exhausted" prose triggers. rebar's OWN `StructuredOutputError`/`UnretryableOutputError` (validation-exhaustion, truncation) are NOT this classifier's inputs — they are handled at the structured seam and pass through `runner.py:351` unchanged.
- **Unwrap `tenacity.RetryError` FIRST** (`RetryError.last_attempt.exception()`) before matching, so an exhausted-retry `httpx.ReadTimeout`/`ConnectTimeout` maps correctly (regression-tested). `import tenacity` is GUARDED (`try/except ImportError`) → a safe no-op in wave 1 (tenacity arrives with story arcticduck/95a5).
- Discriminate `UnexpectedModelBehavior` subclasses (`ContentFilterError`, `IncompleteToolCall`) rather than the base.

### The single metadata-only runner touch (this story)
At the generic `except Exception` seam (`runner.py:353-365`) ONLY, call `classify_llm_failure(exc)` and attach the `LLMOutcome` to the raised `LLMUnavailableError` as an attribute (e.g. `err.outcome`), WITHOUT changing that it raises `LLMUnavailableError`. The `UsageLimitExceeded` arm, the `except LLMError` passthrough, the structured `UnretryableOutputError` path, and `is_context_limit_error` are LEFT EXACTLY AS-IS. Wiring the classifier at the other seams + onto verdict/coverage + exit codes is blackbear's job.

### Diagnostic sanitization (concrete mechanism, not just an ADR)
The diagnostic is built from an ALLOWLIST (exception type, status_code, provider error `code`/`type`, finish_reason, Retry-After, attempts, trace_id, model, provider, request/tool-call-limit flags) — the raw provider body is NEVER dumped wholesale. Any captured free-text passes through a single pure redactor masking `Authorization`/`x-api-key`/`api-key`/`cookie` values, bearer tokens, key-shaped tokens (`sk-...`, `sk-ant-...`, long hex/base64), and emails. Unit-tested against a planted secret. The ADR is RECORDED by blackbear (2eca, sole ADR/docs owner).

## Scope
- The five constructs above, all in `src/rebar/llm/failure.py` (stable path; story drake imports from here — a layout change is a breaking inter-child contract coordinated via blackbear).
- The single metadata-only runner touch (attach `LLMOutcome` at the generic seam; change no raised type).
- Best-effort provider-body inspection for the terminal-vs-transient 429 split (behind the reliable `retryable` core; a misread wastes <= N bounded retries, never a safety issue).
- NOT in scope: any NEW exception classes / raise-site changes / `errors.py` edits (blackbear); the retry mechanism (arcticduck); exit-code mapping, verdict/coverage plumbing, gate posture (blackbear).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `classify_llm_failure` maps every row of the table above to the correct `resolution_class` + `retryable`, pinned by a test that calls it DIRECTLY with each concrete signal (including `UsageLimitExceeded` and `finish_reason=="content_filter"`); an unrecognized exception -> NEEDS_INVESTIGATION.
- [ ] Catches `httpx.*` directly (ConnectTimeout is NOT a ConnectError — asserted); discriminates `ContentFilterError`/`IncompleteToolCall`; unwraps `tenacity.RetryError` first (a test wraps an `httpx.ReadTimeout` in a `RetryError` and asserts WAIT_AND_RETRY), with the `import tenacity` guarded so wave-1 (no tenacity) still passes.
- [ ] This story raises NO new exception type; a test asserts `classify_llm_failure` is additive metadata and that the existing `except LLMUnavailableError` catches + exit-code consumers are unchanged (nothing new is raised, `is_context_limit_error` still True for context-length, `check_stop_reason` still raises `UnretryableOutputError`).
- [ ] The diagnostic is sanitized (a test injects a fake API key / auth header in a provider body and asserts it is scrubbed before any sink), and a `sanitize_diagnostic` failure does not make the classifier raise (degrades to a placeholder).
- [ ] The classifier is total and never raises (property test over arbitrary exceptions, incl. one whose `__str__` raises).
- [ ] The metadata-only runner touch attaches the `LLMOutcome` at the generic seam without altering the raised type; the arawana RED assertions and the sizing/structured tests stay green; `make test`/`make lint`/`make typecheck` green.

Files: `src/rebar/llm/failure.py` (new — all five constructs), `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` (attach `LLMOutcome` at the generic seam only), `tests/unit/test_llm_failure_classifier.py` (new — table + sanitizer + totality + preservation tests).

## Verified library behavior (experiment log — pydantic-ai 1.107.0, tenacity 9.1.4, httpx 0.28.1, offline)
Run offline (ACTUAL captured output; no billable call). Confirms the symbols the classifier
discriminates and the tenacity unwrap the design pins:
```
[1] pydantic_ai.exceptions MROs:
    ModelHTTPError           OK   ['ModelAPIError', 'AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError']
    ModelAPIError            OK   ['AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError', 'Exception']
    ContentFilterError       OK   ['UnexpectedModelBehavior', 'AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError']
    IncompleteToolCall       OK   ['UnexpectedModelBehavior', 'AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError']
    UsageLimitExceeded       OK   ['AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError', 'Exception']
    UserError                OK   ['RuntimeError', 'Exception', 'BaseException']
    FallbackExceptionGroup   OK   ['ExceptionGroup', 'BaseExceptionGroup', 'Exception']
    ContentFilterError <: UnexpectedModelBehavior: True
    IncompleteToolCall <: UnexpectedModelBehavior: True
    FallbackExceptionGroup MRO: ['ExceptionGroup', 'BaseExceptionGroup', 'Exception']
[2] ModelHTTPError.status_code=429 body-error-type='insufficient_quota'  (terminal-vs-transient 429 reads body)
[3] httpx: [ConnectError, ConnectTimeout, ReadTimeout]; ReadTimeout <: TimeoutException: True (caught directly)
[4] tenacity.RetryError.last_attempt.exception() -> httpx.ReadTimeout: read timed out  (the unwrap)
[5] tenacity reraise=True -> httpx.ConnectTimeout (NOT RetryError: True)  (normal transport path)
[6] UsageLimitExceeded <: UnexpectedModelBehavior: False | <: AgentRunError: True  (separate FIX_AGENT_DESIGN bucket)
```
These are pydantic-ai/httpx/tenacity LIBRARY symbols, not rebar symbols — a code-grounding pass
restricted to the rebar snapshot must not read their repo-absence as non-existence (bug
succinct-formable-kite). The RED baseline (story arawana, committed) already pins the CURRENT
collapse these cases flip: HTTP/timeout/refusal/incomplete -> LLMUnavailableError today.
finding: The plan's classifier mapping table assigns UsageLimitExceeded → FIX_AGENT_DESIGN, implying classify_llm_failure will receive a UsageLimitExceeded. But runner.py:335 catches UsageLimitExceeded BEFORE the except Exception seam and re-raises it as LLMRunnerError. The metadata-only runner touch attaches LLMOutcome only at the except Exception seam (runner.py:353-365), so the LLMRunnerError raised from the UsageLimitExceeded path will have NO LLMOutcome attached. The plan partially acknowledges leaving that arm as-is but does not acknowledge this outcome-attachment gap.
location: Section 'What' — 'Metadata-only integration in THIS story' and mapping table row 'UsageLimitExceeded → FIX_AGENT_DESIGN'
suggested_fix: Add a single line to the UsageLimitExceeded arm: `err = LLMRunnerError(...); err.outcome = classify_llm_failure(exc); raise err` — consistent with metadata-only (no type change) and gives the FIX_AGENT_DESIGN classification a path to the raised error.

title: Live-store corruption remediation: drive fsck to zero
plan:
# Live-store remediation: drive fsck to zero (safe, reversible, batched, committed)

## Why
`rebar fsck` on the live store = 2422 SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT + 11 ORPHAN_EVENT + 1 MISSING_CREATE. Fix #1 stopped
new corruption; this cleans the backlog. DESTRUCTIVE on a live, git-backed shared store — safety is first-class.
Consumes NEW sibling deliverables: A1 (tricolour-head-ratfish) ADDS the `.retired` rename + `RETIRED_SUFFIX` +
retire helper; A2 (glandulous-potent-waxwing) ADDS the `include_retired` rebuild path. depends_on A1+A2.

## Repair per fault class
- SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT (2422): a folded source file STILL PRESENT (listed in `source_event_uuids`, never deleted).
  Repair = call A1's retire helper to rename each still-present listed source to `.retired`. NOT a rebuild.
- ORPHAN_EVENT (11): a pre-snapshot event absent from `source_event_uuids`. Routed BY EVENT TYPE (see policy):
  AUTO-RECOVER types -> A2's `reduce_ticket(..., include_retired=True)` rebuild; HUMAN-TRIAGE types -> surfaced,
  NOT auto-rebuilt (the rebuild would pick an order that may be wrong).
- MISSING_CREATE (1): `fsck --repair` logs it, no automatic write; human triage.

## Orphan disposition (a CODE constant, not just prose)
Define two sets in code (enumerating ALL KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES so none is unclassified):
- AUTO-RECOVER (additive/commutative): COMMENT, LINK, UNLINK, TAG_DELTA, COMMITS, BRIDGE_ALERT, REVERT.
- HUMAN-TRIAGE (order-sensitive): STATUS, EDIT, FILE_IMPACT, VERIFY_COMMANDS, SIGNATURE, WORKFLOW_RUN,
  WORKFLOW_STEP, ARCHIVED. Decisions recorded in the PR / a linked ops doc.

## Safety
- GIT-AWARE writes: the tracker is a git worktree. Each batch commits+pushes:
  `git -C <tracker> add -A && git -C <tracker> commit -m 'a3-remediation: batch N/M' && git -C <tracker> push`.
  If a push FAILS (conflict/network/reject), ABORT the run and surface the error — do NOT continue; the pre-tag
  enables rollback. BEFORE any write: `git -C <tracker> tag pre-a3-remediation <oid>`; rollback = `git reset --hard
  pre-a3-remediation && git push --force-with-lease`.
- Dry-run FIRST: `fsck --repair --dry-run` prints per-ticket before/after diff + dispositions, writes NOTHING
  (a test asserts zero file writes + zero commits); the live run is gated on human review.
- Reconciler pause: disable the `reconcile-bridge.yml` GHA schedule for the window via the GitHub API/CLI
  (`gh workflow disable reconcile-bridge.yml`), verify no pass is mid-flight, and RE-ENABLE in a `finally`/failsafe
  (auto re-enable after N minutes or on repair-process exit) so a crash never leaves it disabled. (The pass-lock
  `refs/reconciler/lock` is a leased CAS lock and would expire, so it is NOT the pause mechanism.) Manual writers
  hold off (documented); the batched+committed+pre-tagged design makes a stray write recoverable.
- Idempotent/resumable: fsck itself is the AUTHORITATIVE resumability check (re-run repairs only tickets fsck still
  flags). A per-ticket `.a3-repaired` marker is a LOCAL, gitignored optimization only. Per-snapshot rewrite uses the
  EXISTING `rebar._store.fsutil.atomic_write` (not hand-rolled). The SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT rename + rebuild hold the
  store write-lock for the per-ticket critical section (no TOCTOU with a concurrent writer). Per-ticket failure
  logs+skips (no batch abort). Batched `--limit=200`, wait >=30s between.

## Scope
`_commands/fsck.py` ONLY (`--repair`/`--dry-run`/`--limit`, per-batch git commit+push, disposition constants,
per-snapshot `fsutil.atomic_write`, local marker, write-lock critical section; update the `fsck --help` golden files
`tests/golden/cli_help/fsck__help.out` + the `-h` variant). This ticket does NOT edit `compact.py` (A1 owns all
compact.py changes) — it CALLS A1's retire helper and A2's `include_retired` rebuild. Gated on RC1 fix #1.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `fsck --repair --dry-run` prints per-ticket before/after + dispositions; a test asserts ZERO file writes and ZERO commits in dry-run.
- [ ] Live repair commits+pushes each batch; a push failure ABORTS + surfaces the error; pre-tag created; rollback rehearsed on a fixture (post-rollback store == pre-tag state).
- [ ] Reconciler paused by `gh workflow disable` (verified no in-flight pass), re-enabled in a failsafe `finally`.
- [ ] Orphan disposition is a code constant covering ALL KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES; AUTO-RECOVER rebuilt, HUMAN-TRIAGE surfaced (not auto-rebuilt); tested.
- [ ] Resumability via fsck re-check; `.a3-repaired` marker local/gitignored; per-snapshot rewrite uses `fsutil.atomic_write`; write-lock held per ticket; crash-before-marker is safe (test).
- [ ] Synthetic-fixture unit tests: SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT / ORPHAN_EVENT (both dispositions) / MISSING_CREATE (incl. `.retired`) repair correctly.
- [ ] Live store reaches 0 UNRESOLVED faults (triaged+documented orphans count as resolved); before/after counts recorded.
finding: The plan's Scope lists `_commands/compact.py (retire-still-present path)` as a component to modify. The actual `compact.py` hard-deletes folded source files via `os.remove()` (line 140–143) — there is no rename-to-`.retired` path. The `.retired` mechanism is described in docs (concurrency.md, event-schema.md) as how compaction works, but that is the historical bash implementation (`ticket-compact.sh`). The current Python `compact.py` never produces `*.retired` files.
location: Scope section — '_commands/compact.py (retire-still-present path)'

title: op-cert: trusted gate service — authoritative-state fetch, per-kind dispatch, async job API (+ client command)
plan:
# op-cert: trusted gate service — authoritative-state fetch, per-kind dispatch, async job API (+ client command)

## Why
For high assurance, a developer routes a gate run to a trusted environment that fetches
authoritative state ITSELF (ticket state from the tickets branch; code at the review remote's
`main` tip), runs the gate, and returns an Ed25519-signed `rebar.opcert.v1` cert — so
client-supplied inputs cannot forge a pass (the SLSA non-falsifiable-provenance principle). This
story builds the **service and its client half only**; AWS IaC + the real deploy are the sibling
"AWS IaC + deploy" story, and the signing behavior itself ships in the "producer signing seam"
story this depends on. All design forks are settled in the epic's "Producer-signing &
compute-runtime design resolution" section — this story implements, it does not decide.

**Hard dependencies (not yet on `main`):** the op-cert substrate consumed here
(`signing.sign_opcert_manifest`, `src/rebar/attest/opcert.py`, `trusted_env.py`, `sshsig.sign` —
Gerrit changes 649/650/660, in review), the merge-gate consumer `rebar verify-opcert` (story 4214,
Gerrit change 664, in review — referenced by the client ACs below), and the producer signing seam
story must land first;
mentions of them below are forward references to that stack, enforced by this ticket's
`depends_on` links. `src/rebar/review_bot/app.py` (the pattern mirrored) IS on `main` today.

## What
**Service** (`src/rebar/opcert_service/`, library-mode: rebar imported behind a thin FastAPI app —
the `review_bot/app.py` software pattern; NOT the stdio `rebar-mcp`):
- **Async job API** (gate runs take 30s–minutes; no sync request can hold): `POST /opcert/jobs`
  validates, enqueues on an in-process `asyncio.Queue`, returns **202 `{job_id}`**;
  `GET /opcert/jobs/{job_id}` returns the job record. Single background worker drains serially
  under a bounded per-run timeout: default **900 s** (gate runs take 30s–minutes; the ceiling is
  generous), configurable via `REBAR_OPCERT_JOB_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`. Job state is in-memory (a restart loses queued jobs; clients
  re-submit — cert minting is idempotent).
- **Request schema**: `{"ticket_id": str, "kind": "completion-verifier"|"plan-review"}` — ONLY
  identifiers. Any other client field (a `material_fingerprint`, a `commit`, an `env_id`) is
  IGNORED; the server derives every authoritative value itself.
- **Authoritative fetch**: the worker fetches the tickets branch (ticket state) and the review
  remote's `main` tip (code) with its own read-only credentials — sources named by env config:
  `REBAR_OPCERT_REVIEW_REMOTE_URL` (code) and `REBAR_OPCERT_TICKETS_REMOTE_URL` (tickets
  branch), credentials from the service env. It sets `merged_log_commit = <fetched main HEAD
  sha>` and computes the material fingerprint itself. Each job runs in an **ephemeral
  workspace clone** discarded after the job.
- **Per-kind dispatch + native verdicts (no unified enum)**: `plan-review` →
  `rebar.llm.review_plan(...)` (verdict `PASS|BLOCK|INDETERMINATE`); `completion-verifier` →
  `rebar.llm.verify_completion(...)` (verdict `PASS|FAIL`). Error handling is per-kind and
  ASYMMETRIC, matching the live code: `verify_completion` RAISES
  `LLMError`/`LLMUnavailableError` → the job ends `status: "error"` with the enum mapping
  below; `review_plan` does NOT raise on an LLM outage — it degrades IN-BAND to
  `verdict: "INDETERMINATE"` (unsigned), so the job COMPLETES with that verdict and
  `envelope: null`; only an unexpected exception maps a plan-review job to
  `status: "error", class: "internal"`. Never a fabricated verdict. Response/job schema:
  `{status: "queued"|"running"|"completed"|"error", kind, verdict: <native enum>|null,
  envelope: <encoded DSSE>|null, material_fingerprint, merged_log_commit, error: {class, message}|null}`
  — `envelope` present ONLY on a `PASS` verdict. `error.class` is a CLOSED enum mapped from the
  raised exception type: `"llm_unavailable"` (`LLMUnavailableError`), `"llm_error"` (other
  `LLMError`), `"timeout"` (per-run timeout exceeded), `"internal"` (anything else).
- **Signing = the producer seam, once; the service only PROVISIONS the key.** The gate
  producers mint the op-cert via the signing seam (producer-seam story); the handler itself
  never signs bespokely and never passes `sign=False`. Per-kind producer step: `review_plan`
  signs internally (`sign=True` default). `verify_completion` has NO `sign` parameter — the
  completion producer step lives in the close gate today (`transition_close.py`'s
  `_verdict_manifest` + seam call) — so this story EXTRACTS that pair into a reusable
  `sign_completion_verdict(result, ticket_id, repo_root)` used by BOTH `transition_close.py`
  (behavior unchanged) and this worker on a PASS verdict: the worker invokes the same producer
  function, not a bespoke signer. The hand-off protocol: this story
  adds one override to the seam's key resolution — `REBAR_OPCERT_KEY_PATH` (an explicit key
  file path) takes precedence over the `<tracker>/.opcert-key` genesis. Before dispatching a
  job, the worker fetches the SSM SecureString (`/rebar/prod/opcert-ed25519-key`),
  **materializes it to a 0600 `mkstemp` temp file** (SSHSIG signing shells
  `ssh-keygen -Y sign -f <path>` and needs a file path), sets `REBAR_OPCERT_KEY_PATH` to that
  path and `REBAR_OPCERT_ENV_ID` as the principal for the gate run (the principal is the
  environment's logical id string — it MUST exactly equal the environment's `env_id` entry in
  the consuming project's `.rebar/trusted_environments.yaml`, since it becomes the DSSE keyid
  the merge gate pins on; a non-empty printable token, e.g. `nava-opcert-prod-1`), and **deletes the file in a
  `finally`** — never persisted, and the seam signs exactly once with the provisioned key. The
  key MUST be passphrase-free (`ssh-keygen -Y sign` is non-interactive). The legacy HMAC
  environment key is never provisioned in the server.
- **Stateless / store-read-only — reconciled with the seam's `sign=True`.** Running a gate with
  signing on appends a `SIGNATURE` event to the store it runs against; on the server that store
  is the job's ephemeral workspace clone, with `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` and NO tickets-branch
  write credential — so the event lands only in the discarded workspace, never on the shared
  branch. The worker reads back the freshly-appended record by the NAMED mechanism —
  `show_ticket(ticket_id)["attestations"][kind]` in the ephemeral workspace (the reduced record
  of the just-written `SIGNATURE` event; `opcert.opcert_from_record` decodes its `envelope` +
  bound fields) — and returns the envelope in the response; the CLIENT persists it to the shared tickets branch. Nothing is
  ever pushed by the server (asserted by a test on the workspace remote config). Endpoint authn
  (SigV4) is terminated at API Gateway (the IaC story); the app additionally requires a shared
  secret as defense in depth — same posture as `review_bot`'s check (which uses a `?token=`
  query parameter, `review_bot/app.py`); the opcert app checks an `X-Opcert-Guard` **header**
  instead (value from the `REBAR_OPCERT_GUARD` env var, materialized from SSM by the deploy
  story), because API Gateway injects static request headers on the integration.
- **Container image**: `infra/compose/Dockerfile.opcert` (build context = repo root, per the
  review_bot Dockerfile pattern) installing **OpenSSH ≥ 8.9** and running
  `uvicorn rebar.opcert_service.app:app`.

**Client** (`src/rebar/_commands/remote_cert.py`, CLI `rebar remote-cert <ticket-id> <kind>`).
Wiring is enumerated (the drift gates that bit change 664 before): the `argv[0] ==` intercept
ladder in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` gains the `remote-cert` arm; a help file is added under
`src/rebar/_cli/help/`; `scripts/gen_cli_reference.py`'s curated `INTERCEPT_COMMANDS` dict is
updated in the same change (its set-equality test fails otherwise); and every new
`REBAR_OPCERT_*` env var is regenerated into `docs/env-vars.md` (`scripts/gen_env_registry.py`
scans `src/rebar`; the CI env-var drift gate fails otherwise).
- Reads `verify.opcert_remote_url` (new `VerifyConfig` key, `str | None = None`; unset → clear
  error; nothing remote is ever required for local operation). SigV4-signs the request with
  ambient AWS credentials, submits the job, polls to a terminal status, and on `PASS` **persists
  the returned envelope + bound fields as the `SIGNATURE` event** (the same event shape
  `sign_opcert_manifest` appends; auto-pushed with the tickets branch). Safe because the envelope
  is self-authenticating — tampering with the bound values breaks the signature the merge gate
  (`rebar verify-opcert`) verifies. Non-PASS / error → non-zero exit with the job's error class.

## Scope
- In: the FastAPI service, dispatch/verdict mapping, authoritative fetch, SSM-parameter key
  materialization + the `REBAR_OPCERT_KEY_PATH` seam override, Dockerfile, the `remote-cert` client command, `verify.opcert_remote_url`.
- Out: Terraform/API Gateway/SSM resources + the real deploy + live round-trip
  (the "AWS IaC + deploy" story); the producer signing seam itself (dependency); HMAC removal
  (8f1d).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `POST /opcert/jobs` ACKs 202 `{job_id}` and `GET /opcert/jobs/{id}` reaches a terminal
      status via the background worker; a test drives the round-trip against a fake authoritative
      source with the request/response schemas above.
- [ ] Integrity test (the load-bearing one): a request carrying a **doctored
      `material_fingerprint`** and a doctored `commit` is accepted, the extra fields are ignored,
      and the signed op-cert's bound `material_fingerprint`/`merged_log_commit` equal the values
      the server derived from its own fetch — a client cannot influence any bound value.
- [ ] Per-kind verdict mapping: tests assert `plan-review` jobs surface
      `PASS|BLOCK|INDETERMINATE` natively, `completion-verifier` jobs surface `PASS|FAIL`, a
      raised `LLMError` yields `status="error"` with `verdict=null`, and `envelope` is non-null
      ONLY on `PASS` (BLOCK/FAIL/INDETERMINATE/error all return `envelope: null`).
- [ ] Key materialization: a test asserts the SSM SecureString value is written to a 0600
      `mkstemp` file for the `ssh-keygen -Y sign` invocation and that NO key file remains after
      signing (including on a raised exception — the `finally` path).
- [ ] The server appends no tickets-branch event: a test asserts the store is unchanged after a
      completed job; `rebar remote-cert` persists the returned envelope as a `SIGNATURE` event
      that `verify_signature`/`rebar verify-opcert` then certify, and a tampered envelope is
      rejected (`mismatch`).
- [ ] Offline default preserved: a test asserts every local op-cert sign/verify path works with
      `verify.opcert_remote_url` unset and no network — the remote path is opt-in and its absence
      never blocks a local operation.
- [ ] `infra/compose/Dockerfile.opcert` installs OpenSSH ≥ 8.9 and starts the service; a test
      asserts the Dockerfile declares both.
- [ ] Origin guard: tests assert a request missing the `X-Opcert-Guard` header, or carrying a
      value that does not match `REBAR_OPCERT_GUARD`, is rejected (403) before any work is
      enqueued; a matching header is accepted.
- [ ] Server never pushes: a test asserts the job workspace runs with `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` and
      no push remote/credential, and that the shared store is byte-identical after a completed
      PASS job (the SIGNATURE event exists only in the discarded workspace).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green.
finding: The plan never declares a `pyproject.toml` extra for the opcert service, yet the service needs `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, and `boto3` (for SSM key fetching) at a minimum. The Dockerfile.opcert will need `pip install '.[<extra>]'` analogous to the reviewbot Dockerfile's `pip install '.[agents,reviewbot]'`. Without a named extra, the Dockerfile install command is underspecified and will either install wrong deps or silently fall back to base deps that omit FastAPI.
location: Service section — 'Container image: infra/compose/Dockerfile.opcert'; Scope/In list
suggested_fix: Add `opcert = ["fastapi", "uvicorn[standard]", "boto3"]` to `[project.optional-dependencies]` in `pyproject.toml`, and specify `pip install '.[agents,opcert]'` in the Dockerfile section of the plan.

title: Completion-verifier FAILs must enumerate ALL unmet criteria in one verdict (anti-ratchet)
plan:
## Why
Ticket 3b4e's close FAILed twice with the unmet criteria surfaced piecemeal — round 1 named one coverage gap, round 2 a different one — costing two extra Gerrit/CI cycles (changes 526, 527). A FAIL verdict should be complete: every unmet criterion enumerated with its remediation in one pass, so one remediation round can clear the gate.

## What
- Update the completion-verifier rubric prompt (src/rebar/llm/reviewers/completion_verifier.md) to require exhaustive evaluation: assess EVERY acceptance/success/close criterion independently and, on FAIL, emit one finding per unmet criterion — never stop at the first failure. State explicitly that a verdict naming only a subset of unmet criteria is an incomplete verdict.
- This is a pass-4/prompt-level change: no verdict-reconciliation or threshold logic changes (rebar.llm.completion.reconcile_verdict stays byte-identical).
- Prove the behavior with an EVAL case, not a tautological FakeRunner test (a FakeRunner test only returns pre-loaded findings, so it cannot demonstrate the prompt achieves exhaustive evaluation). This spans TWO files that must stay mutually consistent (`validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` requires every deterministic scorer named in a spec to be registered in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY`):
  - `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py`: IMPLEMENT and REGISTER a new deterministic scorer `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` in `REGISTRY` — given a case annotated with the set of independently-unmet criteria, it scores 1.0 iff the FAIL verdict emits a distinct finding for EACH (>= N findings, each naming a different unmet criterion), else 0.0.
  - `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/completion-verifier.eval.yaml`: reference that scorer AND add a dataset case `CV-two-unmet-criteria` (expect: fail) whose fixture ticket carries exactly two independent AC gaps (e.g. an unimplemented function AND an untested path). ANNOTATION SCHEMA (code-grounded — existing deterministic scorers read annotation fields DIRECTLY from the raw case dict; there is NO `scorer_args` field, do not invent one): the case dict carries a top-level key `expected_unmet_criteria: [<substr1>, <substr2>]` — a list of short substrings, one per criterion that must appear. The `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer reads `case["expected_unmet_criteria"]` directly (the same way existing scorers read their per-case fields from the case dict) and scores 1.0 iff there is a BIJECTIVE match — each expected substring is matched (case-insensitive substring test on a finding's `criterion` field) to a DISTINCT finding, no finding reused for two substrings — else 0.0. Missing/empty annotation → the scorer abstains (not applicable), consistent with the other deterministic scorers.
  CLOSE-GATE vs CONFIRMATORY (timing, explicit): the eval RUN is a LIVE-LLM eval that runs in the dedicated `[eval]` eval-CI pipeline (the `external`/eval tier, NOT the per-PR `make test` gate — it needs credentials + the [eval] extra and is not part of the Verified vote). So this ticket's CLOSE is gated by the OFFLINE evidence only — `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` passing (which requires the scorer registered) PLUS the FakeRunner plumbing unit test (both run in `make test`). The eval run is the CONFIRMATORY behavioral proof of the prompt behavior, recorded when the eval pipeline next runs; it is not a per-PR gate and does not block the close.
- Additionally add a FakeRunner unit test (mirror tests/interfaces/store/test_completion_verifier.py) asserting the PLUMBING invariant: a multi-finding FAIL verdict survives reconcile_verdict and the close path with all findings intact (guards against regression in the pass-through, distinct from the prompt-behavior eval above).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The completion-verifier prompt (completion_verifier.md, Verdict & findings section) requires per-criterion exhaustive evaluation and one finding per unmet criterion on FAIL, and states that a verdict naming only a subset of unmet criteria is an incomplete verdict.
- [ ] The `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` deterministic scorer is implemented AND registered in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` (evals/eval_scorers.py), and completion-verifier.eval.yaml references it plus a `CV-two-unmet-criteria` case (two independently-unmet criteria). Offline proof: `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` passes on the extended spec (which it can only do because the scorer is registered). Behavioral proof (confirmatory, in the [eval] eval-CI pipeline — NOT the per-PR close gate): the eval run shows the case's FAIL verdict emits a distinct finding for BOTH unmet criteria (scorer == 1.0). The close is gated by the offline validate + the plumbing unit test above, both in `make test`.
- [ ] A FakeRunner unit test asserts a two-finding FAIL verdict is preserved (all findings intact) through reconcile_verdict + the close path.
- [ ] reconcile_verdict and the close-path logic are byte-unchanged (no reconciliation/threshold edits).
- [ ] make lint passes on the change.
- [ ] make test passes on the change.
